pr.title: Replace hardcoded pi values with M_PI
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Replaced many obvious hardcoded pi-related constants with the standard M_PI expression form across fighter, stage, and vector-library code. The PR removed local PI, PI64, and HALF_PI64-style defines where they were clearly equivalent, switched angle normalization and facing-direction logic to M_PI, 2 * M_PI, and M_PI / 2, and included math.h where needed. The author explicitly limited the pass to no-brainer cases and left float-typed degree-to-radian constants and other bit-sensitive cases for future discussion.
what_changed: Removed local hardcoded pi aliases such as PI64, HALF_PI64, PI, and a local M_PI define where the code could use M_PI directly.
Updated Fox blaster launch-angle mirroring from PI64 - angle to M_PI - angle in ftFox_SpecialN.c.
Updated Game & Watch and Luigi Super Jump / rescue facing-direction angle calls from HALF_PI64 casts to (M_PI / 2) * fp->x2C_facing_direction.
Changed Mario's HALF_PI macro to derive from M_PI / 2, while leaving DEGREES_TO_RADIANS as a float-typed hardcoded expression.
Changed Master Hand's -3.1415927410125732f call argument to -M_PI.
Changed Mewtwo Shadow Ball release facing direction from PI64 to M_PI.
Reworked many Ness PK Thunder constants: pi, half-pi, two-pi, negative half-pi, degrees-to-radians, and small epsilon-like literals were expressed as M_PI-derived or simpler decimal forms.
Updated lbvector's internal sin/cos approximation angle wrapping to use M_PI and 2 * M_PI instead of a local PI define.
Changed grshrine.c from a file-local M_PI define to including math.h.
smart_moves: Scoped the PR to obvious source-level replacements instead of attempting a full global pass over every pi-like literal.
Preferred standard math.h M_PI over proliferating file-local PI, PI64, HALF_PI64, and duplicate M_PI defines.
Used expressions such as M_PI / 2 and 2 * M_PI to make angle intent clearer than long decimal literals.
Avoided over-consolidating known tricky float-typed constants after review discussion raised bitwise matching concerns.
Removed constants from headers when they were only serving as generic math aliases, reducing header pollution.
decomp_lessons: Hardcoded pi literals can often be replaced with M_PI-derived expressions, but only when the resulting type and bit pattern remain compatible with matching requirements.
Double-precision-looking literals such as 3.141592653589793 and 1.5707963267948966 are stronger candidates for M_PI cleanup than f32-specific literals.
Float-typed constants like 3.1415927410125732f or degree-to-radian constants may need a macro, inline function, or separate consolidation plan to preserve codegen.
Do not assume every pi-like decimal has the same source-level origin; some may come from compiler-expanded macros, inline conversions, or float rounding.
When replacing local math constants, make sure the file has access to M_PI through math.h or project headers.
naming_conventions: M_PI is preferred for the canonical pi constant rather than local PI or PI64 defines.
Use expression forms for common derived constants: M_PI / 2 for half-pi, 2 * M_PI for tau/two-pi, and -M_PI / 2 for negative half-pi.
Legacy names observed and removed or reduced include PI64, HALF_PI64, PI, and file-local M_PI.
DEGREES_TO_RADIANS remained as a named macro in Mario code, with discussion suggesting future consolidation rather than ad hoc replacement.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Treat f32 and f64 pi literals differently; replacing a float literal with M_PI can alter casts or emitted constants if not handled carefully.
Reviewers explicitly questioned whether M_PI / 180.0f was bitwise equivalent to the existing DEGREES_TO_RADIANS macro.
For matching-sensitive degree-to-radian conversions, consider a macro or inline that preserves float typing instead of directly substituting M_PI.
Parenthesized M_PI-derived expressions were used to preserve arithmetic grouping, e.g. (M_PI / 2) * fp->x2C_facing_direction.
Simplifying literals such as 9.999999747378752e-05f to 1e-4F can improve readability when it is a known float representation, but should still be checked against generated code.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Asked whether replacing DEGREES_TO_RADIANS with M_PI / 180.0f would be bitwise different from the existing define.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Noted uncertainty around float-typed degree-to-radian constants and suggested consolidating them later via macro or inline.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Suggested a possible full pass using a GAS .define for assembly and a C #define.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Clarified that this PR intentionally handled only no-brainer source cases; nontrivial f32-typed values require discussion.

follow_up_queries: Find remaining hardcoded pi, half-pi, two-pi, and degree-to-radian literals that were not covered by PR #539.
Audit f32-typed pi constants such as 3.1415927410125732f for whether they should be represented by macros or inline helpers.
Search assembly files for pi literals that could use a GAS .define, as suggested in review.
Consolidate duplicate DEGREES_TO_RADIANS constants while preserving bitwise matching.
Verify which files rely on M_PI through transitive includes versus direct #include <math.h>.
